title: Simple Panzanella Salad

Perfect when tomatoes are in season and you have some stale white bread.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4p74e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"46t0u","text":"This version is simplified; please see the NYT recipe for a fancier meal.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"8225BE40321F11EDB09DDB6012155C1B","url":"https://cooking.nytimes.com/recipes/1017518-panzanella-with-mozzarella-and-herbs?ds_c=71700000052595478\u0026gclid=Cj0KCQjwjvaYBhDlARIsAO8PkE36HkCGvvDEbizPPonRCMR0nITKTHrsDKE8bvAEjZypui2zej1QiLEaAjf6EALw_wcB\u0026gclsrc=aw.ds"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"d5h75","text":"1/3 of a ciabatta loaf, cut into cubes (baguette or other stale bread, even bagels work!), 3-4 cups cut salt","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"acmh3","text":"2lbs ripe steps: Cut up the bread in 1-inch cubes and toast at 425 degrees for ~10 minutes. Let them cool.
Place cut up tomatoes in a large salad bowl.
Add cut/torn mozzarella, sliced red onion, minced garlic.

Add: 2 tablespoon red wine vinegar oregano or thyme 1/2 teaspoon of salt 6 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil black pepper to taste Mix and let sit for a few minutes so that the tomatoes release the juice.
Chop or slice cucumbers and cut up basil and parsley. Add them to the bowl.
Add the bread cubes and let the salad sit at room temperature for 30min to couple of hours.
creator: Lenny Teytelman
